impact_on_program.next: Forbid/pay multi-mates at long scales inside residual, then tight-clique ≤18; or M_m^res≤1; or U_phi
impact_on_program.reduction: Long multi-mates at deployed m ⇔ multi-mates at some s in [67473, 913632] at same depth w

deployed.note: Deployed tight scale e=w+1 does not divide n; pure cosets use e0=2^17 > e.

atomic_scale.name: tight_scale_is_atomic_free1
proof: free = s − w = 1 at s = w+1; apply free-1 packing (v6).
Cosets when e0 = w+1 divides n: pencil size n/e0 (v8).
atomic_scale.statement: The minimal multi-mate scale s = w+1 is exactly the free-1 regime for block size s: M_{w+1}^{max}(w) ≤ floor(n/(w+1)), achieved by full free-1 CS pencils (e.g. pure cosets when (w+1)|n).

block_factorization.name: multimate_block_factorization
proof: From v9/v12: |R| = |C ∩ C'| ≤ m−w−1, so s = m−|R| ≥ w+1.
Disjoint unions C = R ⊔ U, C' = R ⊔ V give the locator factorizations.
Phi_w(C)=Phi_w(C') means deg(Λ_C − Λ_{C'}) ≤ m−w−1. But Λ_C − Λ_{C'} = Λ_R (Λ_U − Λ_V), so deg = |R| + deg(Λ_U − Λ_V) ≤ m−w−1, hence deg(Λ_U − Λ_V) ≤ m−w−1−(m−s) = s−w−1.
For monic degree-s locators, deg(difference) ≤ s−w−1 means the first w high monic coefficients agree, i.e. Phi_w(U)=Phi_w(V).

long_free_criterion.name: no_long_scales_implies_only_tight
long_free_criterion.note: Still a large family of scales. The point is reduction: residual uniqueness at ALL free≥2 scales of depth w implies only tight multi-mates at deployed m. Entropy at each scale with C(n,s)/p^w ≪ 1 is the same style of claim.
proof: If a multi-mate had s ≥ w+2 then M_s^{max}(w) ≥ 2, contradiction.
Hence only s = w+1 multi-mates remain (or no multi-mates).
long_free_criterion.statement: If M_s^{max}(w) ≤ 1 for every s ∈ [w+2, m], then every multi-mate pair at (m,w) has block size s = w+1 (tight only). If moreover every residual multi-mate fiber is a pairwise-tight clique, then M_m^{res,phi} ≤ k_tight = 18, and U_res ≤ floor(target/(17*18)) closes the residual atom.

program.name: v13_reduction
program.statement: Residual atom after v12–v13 reduces to forbidding multi-mates at long scales s ∈ [w+2, m] inside residual can-cores (or paying them), then applying tight-clique packing ≤ 18; or proving residual M_m^{res} ≤ 1 directly; or bounding U_phi.

recursion.name: multimate_scale_recursion
proof: (A) Block factorization produces U ≠ V with Phi_w(U)=Phi_w(V), |U|=s.
(B) v9 padding reduction / v8 coset case.
recursion.statement: Let M_m^{max}(w) be the max Phi_w fiber size among m-subsets of D. (A) If there exists a multi-mate pair at (m,w) with block size s, then M_s^{max}(w) ≥ 2. (B) Conversely, if M_s^{max}(w) ≥ 2 and n − s ≥ m − s (i.e. n ≥ m) with room for a common pad R of size m−s disjoint from two disjoint s-blocks in one fiber, then M_m^{max}(w) ≥ 2 (padding reduction, v8/v9). (C) Therefore long multi-mates at (m,w) exist only if multi-mates exist at some scale s ∈ [w+2, m].

scale_dictionary.name: scale_dictionary_tight_vs_long
proof: Immediate from block factorization + trade-weight law (v12) + tight-pair law (v9).
free_s = s − w: when s = w+1, free_s = 1; when s ≥ w+2, free_s ≥ 2.

title: Recursive block factorization of Phi_w multi-mates
